description: Fix missing defensive mapping on Slack API response fields in message-reading tools. Three tools (read-messages, read-thread, search-messages) access API response fields directly without null/undefined guards while their output schemas declare those fields as required strings. When Slack returns system messages with missing fields (channel_join, bot_message, deleted-channel matches), the tool output violates the schema contract — the AI agent receives undefined or the tool crashes with a TypeError.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/slack && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add defensive defaults in read-messages and read-thread message mapping
userStories.description: As a plugin consumer, I want read_messages and read_thread to return well-formed message objects even when Slack returns system messages with missing fields, so that the AI agent always receives data matching the output schema.
acceptanceCriteria: In read-messages.ts, the message mapping (lines 53-58) applies defensive defaults: type defaults to 'message', text defaults to '', ts defaults to ''
In read-thread.ts, the message mapping (lines 45-51) applies the same defensive defaults: type defaults to 'message', text defaults to '', ts defaults to ''
The data.messages access in both files has a fallback to empty array (data.messages ?? []) to guard against the field being absent
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- plugins/slack/src/tools/read-messages.ts (lines 53-58): Change the message map to add defaults:
  ```
  messages: (data.messages ?? []).map(m => ({
    type: m.type ?? 'message',
    user: m.user,
    text: m.text ?? '',
    ts: m.ts ?? '',
  })),
  ```
- plugins/slack/src/tools/read-thread.ts (lines 45-51): Same pattern:
  ```
  messages: (data.messages ?? []).map(m => ({
    type: m.type ?? 'message',
    user: m.user,
    text: m.text ?? '',
    ts: m.ts ?? '',
    thread_ts: m.thread_ts,
  })),
  ```

The `user` field is already z.string().optional() in the messageSchema (channel-schema.ts:31), so it does NOT need a default. Only `type`, `text`, and `ts` are required strings in the schema.

Follow the same defensive mapping pattern used in list-channels.ts which calls mapChannel() with defaults for every required field.

userStories.title: Add defensive defaults in search-messages match mapping
userStories.description: As a plugin consumer, I want search_messages to return well-formed match objects even when Slack returns matches for messages from deleted channels, so that the tool doesn't crash with a TypeError and the AI agent always receives schema-conformant data.
acceptanceCriteria: In search-messages.ts, the match mapping (lines 90-98) guards against m.channel being undefined: channel.id and channel.name default to empty strings
The match mapping applies defaults for username (defaults to ''), text (defaults to ''), ts (defaults to ''), permalink (defaults to '')
The messages.matches access (line 90) retains its existing fallback to empty array
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
